creator: alt2600@icloud.com
text:
(In reply to George Mitchell from comment #7)

I didn't have any issues with cython in my 14.2 upgrade. I wonder if you are hitting an issue I had several update cycles ago. I have found site-packages folder ends up with stray files sometimes that completely break rebuilds after updates. Don't know why the files are stray, I suspect pkg issues/bugs since I never scrubbed until I couldn't go to 13 or 13.1 I forget now, or interrupted installs/deletes and the like. Not sure how you do your updates, but for source updates I know make sure after deleting all ports after I have my rebuild list ready i basically rm -rf /usr/local/lib/python3.11 or whatever version as applicable. clean out some other folders in local similarly, to clean up any possibly orphaned directories from long deleted ports or version. 

this was the smoothest rebuild I've had ever I think, only a cron job stopped a rebuild of 2400 ports by seemingly locking pkg db on me. I was using amd64, so not sure if cython has other arch issues. now disabling periodic cron jobs is on the list for the next go. 

that being said I know gentoo only seems to have cython 3, would be nice to only have one, or at least not end up in a world where slowly ports migrate over, and big things like KDE stop working, or just general python being more and more incompatible with python within ports. Its one thing in my case losing cad packages, but to lose a desktop environment, I'd almost consider looking at linux again after 20 years.
